verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY I’d do a quick Surfline and MEO Beachcam check before heading out, because Rey Cortiço is only really “maybe” in the mid-morning window. The waves could line up, but the wind setup looks off for that spot and Surfline consistency is pretty poor, so it could easily be choppy. Tide’s dropping, which can still leave a little chance if the sandbanks line up, but I’m keeping it conditional—only go if the cams look noticeably cleaner than expected. Otherwise, save the drive.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 11 kJ, 8 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0-0.3 m, energy not captured, 4 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and weak over the Baleal sandbanks; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and weak over the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes this one less convincing.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
